question: v1 forensics vocabulary: the proposed six metrics (step/tool distribution, edit-test cadence, thrash rate, time-to-first-test, error-recovery latency, destructive-command count) and four detectors (holdout-tamper, hardcoded-expected-output, test-skip insertion, suspicious single-step completion)?
author: claude/design

question: LLM forensic review pass: in v1 (reusing EVAL-9's blinded, isolated, kappa-calibrated pattern) or deferred to a follow-up slice, shipping the deterministic tier alone first?
author: claude/design

question: Gaming-flag disposition: disclose-only plus a ledgered operator quarantine path, or auto-fail/auto-quarantine flagged trials?
author: claude/design

question: Fence coupling: should any forensic flag refuse the official render in v1, or is forensics disclosure-only until detector precision is established via calibration?
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: all EVAL-10/11/12 recommendations accepted. Six metrics + four detectors as specced; the vocabulary is closed and versioned so additions are deliberate version bumps, not drift
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. EVAL-9's blinded/isolated/kappa-calibrated pattern already exists to reuse; shipping the LLM pass alongside the deterministic tier lets calibration data accumulate from the first experiment
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. Flags are evidence, never verdicts: auto-failing on an unproven detector would bias the instrument it protects; the operator path is ledgered so no disposition is invisible
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. A detector must prove its precision through calibration before it can block an official finding; fence coupling is revisited as a new decision once kappa data exists
author: jyang

question: destructive_command_count is unmeasurable from the landed v1 TrajectoryRecord (steps carry no command content): ship the metric as an honest always-null in v1, or extend TrajectoryStep with an optional command field (EVAL-12 contract, schema_version bump to 2)?
author: claude/build

rationale: Build session 2026-07-04: extend now rather than ship a permanently-null metric. Additive Optional[str] command on TrajectoryStep, TRAJECTORY_SCHEMA_VERSION 1 -> 2; empty string = measured-not-a-shell-command (the codex files=[] precedent), null = unmeasurable; v1 records load under the v2 model with command null throughout, so the metric is null for pre-change trials and no reader requires the field
migration: v1 records validate unchanged (field optional); metrics treat any null command among executed steps as unmeasurable -> null metric
author: jyang

question: AC-4 calibrates the LLM forensic pass against human spot-checks, but no write path for human forensic labels exists: a bench forensics record subcommand (EVAL-9 process-record mirror), extending the EVAL-7 review packet, or function-only kappa with ingestion deferred?
author: claude/build

rationale: Build session 2026-07-04: mirror EVAL-9's judge/human dual path inside the forensics subsystem. One ledgered forensic_spotcheck event per human record carrying per-detector labels and the reviewer's stratum, so the existing IPW ReviewedItem machinery applies unchanged
author: jyang

question: D003's ledgered operator quarantine path: does quarantining a trial exclude its data from comparisons (with prominent disclosure), or is the event disclosure-only with no computational effect?
author: claude/build

rationale: Build session 2026-07-04: a disposition that changes nothing is not a disposition. Quarantine is a ledgered human action (never automatic): the trial's grade/judge/process data leaves the comparisons and every render discloses the exclusion with trial id, reason, and actor. Flags themselves still gate nothing [D004]
author: jyang
